creators.name: Nick Saraev
creators.blurb: Practical AI automation and agency builds from someone shipping real systems.

videos.title: I Built a $1M/y SaaS with Claude Code, Here's How
videos.summary: Nick Saraev describes building Clairvo, a $1M/year SaaS that uses Claude Code-generated techniques like predictive pacing and local number rotation to improve call pickup rates. He argues that using Claude to enumerate opportunities and practical implementation of AI agents is viable for building real-world revenue-generating businesses, contrasting his approach with many who talk automation without results.

videos.title: How to Build Mobile Apps with Claude Code: Full Course (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev's full-length course teaches mobile app development using Claude Code and Expo/React Native. The course covers setup, app design frameworks, building three complete apps (habit tracker, calorie tracker with AI, Pomodoro timer), database integration with Supabase, user authentication, and app store submission.

videos.title: How to Use Claude Code for FREE (2026)
videos.summary: Nick Saraev demonstrates how to use Claude Code for free or at minimal cost by routing through NVIDIA-NIM, OpenRouter, Ollama for local models, and alternatives like DeepSeek V4 Flash. The video includes setup instructions for each method and references a full 4-hour Claude Code course.

videos.title: Claude Opus-4.7 Just Dropped, And...
videos.summary: Claude Opus-4.7 is the latest version of Anthropic's Claude model. Nick Saraev discusses the release and its applications in a YouTube video covering practical automation workflows and business use cases.

creators.name: AI Engineer
creators.blurb: Talks and sessions from the AI Engineer conference and community.

videos.title: Why Can't Anyone Answer Questions About the Business? — Garrett Galow, WorkOS
videos.summary: WorkOS built Studio, an internal workspace that answers business questions in natural language by querying Snowflake, Linear, and Notion without manual engineer involvement. The tool uses LLMs to generate reusable declarative JavaScript widgets that call data sources directly, making subsequent runs deterministic and cost-effective.

videos.title: The agent-ready web: Simplify user actions with WebMCP — Tara Agyemang, Google
videos.summary: WebMCP is a proposed web standard that exposes structured tools to AI agents instead of requiring DOM scraping and pixel-coordinate clicking. It provides both declarative HTML-attribute-based APIs and imperative JavaScript APIs for registering typed, named actions that agents can call directly. The feature is in early preview on Chrome 146.

videos.title: Your Attention Is the Bottleneck, Not Your Agents — Zack Proser, WorkOS
videos.summary: Zack Proser argues that human attention, not agent speed, is the limiting factor in AI-augmented development. He proposes a four-layer stack: signal agents for passive monitoring, verification gates for safety, conversation history analysis for self-improvement, and holistic integration with wearables to sustain developer well-being.

videos.title: Stop Making Models Bigger, Make Them Behave — Kobie Crawford, Snorkel
videos.summary: Kobie Crawford demonstrates that small models fine-tuned with RL to develop tool discipline and self-correction behaviors outperform larger models on structured query tasks. A 4B model trained for under $500 correctly retrieved YouTube ad revenue data by first inspecting table schemas and self-correcting on errors, while a 235B parameter model hallucinated answers after failed queries.

creators.name: Cole Medin
creators.blurb: Practical AI agent and coding-assistant builds, end to end.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 is Now BANNED?!
videos.summary: Cole Medin discusses the removal of Claude Fable 5 shortly after launch, reportedly due to US government export-control directives and claimed security vulnerabilities. He reviews Fable 5's performance metrics against Claude Opus 4.8 and reflects on implications for developers building on frontier models.

videos.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
videos.summary: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source command-line tool that enables rapid development and production deployment of AI agents with built-in sandboxing, audit trails, and identity management. The tool integrates with AI coding assistants like Claude and provides a skills-based system for composing agent capabilities.

videos.title: Live AI Q&A + Crushing it in Chess at the Same Time - Come Hang Out!
videos.summary: Cole Medin is hosting a live AI Q&A stream on Saturdays at 9 AM CT covering topics like AI coding, agents, Claude Code, and Archon. The session doubles as casual hangout entertainment with chess play, and the community is organized through Dynamous.

videos.title: Claude Plans, Gemini Designs: The Workflow to Build BEAUTIFUL Frontends
videos.summary: Cole Medin presents a workflow for building frontends by routing planning and reasoning tasks to Claude Opus and UI design tasks to Gemini 3.5 Flash. The approach leverages each model's strengths: Opus for planning and integrations, Gemini for visual design, then Opus for implementation, using tools like Pi and Archon to orchestrate the handoff process.

creators.name: AI Jason
creators.blurb: AI app and agent builds, experiments, and walkthroughs.

videos.title: How to build proactive agents & self-improving company (Fully explained)
videos.summary: AI Jason presents a guide to building proactive agents and self-improving systems. The video covers proactive AI loops, autonomous agent design patterns including autonomous ads optimization and SEO, and highlights Loopany as an open-source framework that enables these capabilities through memory and tool integration.

videos.title: Ralph-loop 2.0? The real autonomous coder is coming...
videos.summary: AI Jason discusses the /goal feature and its potential as an autonomous coder, comparing it to Ralph-loop 2.0. The video covers how the /goal feature works, its setup, prompting practices, goal-buddy functionality, and /Mission exploration for extended task execution.

videos.title: New AI coding paradiagm - OpenAI Symphony
videos.summary: OpenAI Symphony is a new AI coding framework that integrates with developer tooling for automated testing and workflow management. The video demonstrates setup procedures including harness configuration and WORKFLOW.md integration.

videos.title: Okay, this unleashed my agent
videos.summary: AI Jason presents a cheat sheet and overview of AI agent architectures, focusing on Autoresearch and AutoAgent frameworks. The video compares memory systems used in Claude Code, the self-evolution mechanisms in Hermes and Openclaw, highlighting different approaches to building and improving autonomous agents.

creators.name: Caleb Writes Code
creators.blurb: Hands-on coding sessions and software build-alongs.

videos.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
videos.summary: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series comprising Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The series features a distinctive architecture combining hybrid mamba transformer, latent mixture of experts, and multi-token prediction techniques to differentiate from other open models.

videos.title: MiniMax M3 explained in 8min..
videos.summary: MiniMax released M3, a language model that introduces MSA (MiniMax Sparse Attention) to replace full attention mechanisms. The model incorporates tiled and I/O optimizations including optimized KV cache reading and improvements to prefill and decode stage inference performance.

videos.title: Pi Agent explained in 6min..
videos.summary: A video explanation of Pi Agent, an AI agent framework that enables unique approaches to task harnessing. The video compares Pi against other agent systems like Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, and Antigravity, discussing how it handles the complexity of harness layers in modern AI products.

videos.title: Huawei makes 1.4nm chip??
videos.summary: Caleb Writes Code discusses Huawei's announcement of Tau Scaling Law as a competitive response in the global semiconductor race. The video covers the role of EUV and DUV lithography, packaging, and logic folding in chip advancement amid US-China technology competition.

creators.name: AWS Developers
creators.blurb: Official AWS tutorials, service deep dives, and developer demos.

videos.title: AI and the Job Market in 2026
videos.summary: AWS Developers discusses strategies for standing out in the 2026 job market amid AI screening. The video covers networking effectiveness versus mass applications, personal branding, the Feynman Principle for learning, and argues that junior developers will evolve but not be replaced, using a transportation analogy for how roles adapt rather than disappear.

videos.title: 5 Tips for Deploying AI Agents to Production
videos.summary: AWS Developers presents five production best practices for TypeScript AI agents: streaming tool call events for UI responsiveness, layered authentication with API Gateway and Cognito, type-safe contracted database access with tenant isolation, guardrails and cost budgets via lifecycle hooks, and comprehensive observability through OpenTelemetry tracing and built-in metrics. The guidance emphasizes patterns that apply across platforms but demonstrates them on AWS infrastructure.

videos.title: Infrastructure as Code for Beginners (AWS CDK)
videos.summary: A YouTube tutorial from AWS Developers on Infrastructure as Code using AWS CDK with Python. The video demonstrates building a serverless REST API by comparing manual AWS console setup against declarative infrastructure automation, covering DynamoDB, Lambda, and API Gateway with practical deployment and teardown.

videos.title: Stop AI Hallucinations With These 5 Techniques
videos.summary: AWS Developers video presents five techniques for reducing AI hallucinations and token waste in agent systems: semantic tool selection to filter context, graph RAG for precise structured queries, multi-agent validation to catch failures, symbolic guardrails via Python code hooks, and runtime steering for self-correction. Demonstrations use Strands Agents framework and Amazon Bedrock AgentCore at scale.

creators.name: Visual AI
creators.blurb: Visual walkthroughs and demos across modern AI tools.

creators.name: Alberta Tech
creators.blurb: Developer tutorials and practical builds in AI and software.

videos.title: Do Google engineers actually vibe code?
videos.summary: Alberta Tech explores whether Google engineers engage in "vibing code," a colloquial term for rapid or intuitive coding. The video compares code generation approaches from OpenAI Codex and Claude, then examines Google's vibing culture across 2024, 2025, and 2026 projections, including discussion of "Google Tech Island."

videos.title: This company killed open source
videos.summary: Alberta Tech discusses Malus, a company or service that the video frames as potentially ending open source. The video covers how Malus works, its impact on open source maintainers, and debates whether the company is acting unethically. Chapters range from defining open source concepts to examining Malus's role in the ecosystem.

videos.title: I let Claude Code take my job (it did better than me)
videos.summary: Alberta Tech conducted an experiment comparing Claude Code's software development performance against their own work and found the AI outperformed them. The video explores whether AI tools like Claude Code will replace human developers across all job functions.

creators.name: EO
creators.blurb: Conversations and stories with founders and builders in tech.

videos.title: How to Steal Customers From Giants 600x Bigger than Your Startup | Pigment, Eléonore Crespo
videos.summary: Eléonore Crespo, co-founder of Pigment, discusses how a Paris-based startup with an AI performance management platform competes against enterprise giants 600x larger. She explains why companies like Google rely on spreadsheets for strategic planning, the advantages of building a full enterprise platform from day one, and her hiring philosophy focused on raising organizational standards.

videos.title: The Fastest Way to Know if Your Product Market Fit Is Real | Serval CEO, Jake Stauch
videos.summary: Jake Stauch, CEO of Serval, argues that passionate customer enthusiasm does not guarantee real product-market fit, and that true market fit is characterized by pull demand rather than push sales. He advocates skipping formal customer interviews in favor of embedded relationships and emphasizes betting on the future trajectory of AI models rather than current capabilities.

videos.title: He Raised $26M Betting on Probabilities, Not Guesses | Koah Labs, Nic Baird
videos.summary: Nic Baird, founder of Koah Labs, argues that ad-based monetization is a better model than subscriptions for AI tools and that human trust adoption, not technology, is the primary bottleneck limiting AI agent deployment. He also discusses applying probabilistic decision-making from competitive sailing to startup strategy.

videos.title: I Hit $1M ARR in 117 Days. Bootstrapped to $10M. Here's My Playbook | Chatbase, Yasser Elsaid
videos.summary: Yasser Elsaid, founder of Chatbase, shares his bootstrapping strategy that achieved $1M ARR in 117 days and grew to $10M without external funding. The episode covers decision-making frameworks, product-led growth, SEO strategy, pricing experimentation, and customer retention tactics.

creators.name: Cyril Imhof
creators.blurb: AI automation and workflow builds for real projects.

videos.title: Claude Fable 5 Now Banned in Europe by US Government (EU-Breakdown)
videos.summary: A video by Cyril Imhof discusses the claimed ban of Claude Fable 5 in Europe by the US government. The video references regulatory developments concerning Anthropic's model access across EU jurisdictions.

videos.title: The Ultimate Second Brain for AI Entrepreneurs: Fable 5 Claude with Loops
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Fable 5, an Anthropic model designed for AI entrepreneurs, highlighting its features, guardrails, memory capabilities, and agentic payment integration compared to Mythos 5.

videos.title: Anthropic's Brand New AI Just Started the Gold Rush (Fable 5)
videos.summary: Cyril Imhof discusses Anthropic's new Claude Fable 5 model and its applications in regulated industries. The video covers new use cases for the model and introduces "Know Your Agent" methodology for effective context engineering in long-running AI applications.

creators.name: PY
creators.blurb: Software engineering, tooling, and developer-workflow deep dives.
